NEW YORK, Oct. 26 (Bloomberg) -- Japan's Mitsui & Co. Ltd. said it will buy U.S. energy-trading firm Enron Corp. for $1.1 billion, the biggest foreign acquisition in the U.S. energy industry.

Mitsui, which is based in Tokyo, said it will pay $1.1 billion for Enron's energy trading unit, EnronOnline, and will assume $1.1 billion in debt.

The deal is the biggest foreign acquisition in the U.S. energy industry, and the biggest foreign acquisition in the U.S. energy industry since the U.S. energy company Dynegy Inc. bought Dynegy Energy Inc. in 2001.

The deal is expected to close in the first quarter of 2001, the companies said.

Mitsui said it will pay $1.1 billion in cash and $1.1 billion in shares of Enron.

Enron said it will pay $1.1 billion in cash and $1.1 billion in shares of Mitsui
